output.summary: Applied three bug fixes to the Text block: added missing `not-discourse` class to TSX wrapper, added `shouldDisplay`/`hasContent` conditional for ThemeHeading in TSX to match PHP behavior, and added PHP docblock annotations for $children and $block variables. Created test page with 7 variations covering all scenarios. All validations pass.

changes: Added `not-discourse` class to useBlockProps className to match PHP template
Added `hasContent` boolean computation matching PHP logic (checks eyebrow, heading, description, buttons with url/postId)
Wrapped ThemeHeading in `{ (displayContent || hasContent) && ... }` conditional so it shows when block is selected (for authoring) but hides when deselected and empty (matching PHP frontend behavior)
Added comprehensive PHP docblock comment at the top documenting all available variables
Added @var annotations for: anchor, eyebrow, heading, description, buttons, children, and block

testPage.title: TEST: Text
variations: Full Content - All fields populated with rich text inner blocks
Minimal - Heading Only - Tests hasContent conditional
No ThemeHeading Content - Tests hidden left column when hasContent is false
Long Content - Sticky Test - Tests md:sticky md:top-8 behavior
Eyebrow and Heading Only - No Description or Buttons
With Anchor ID - Tests anchor attribute deep linking
Rich Inner Blocks - Multiple Content Types (headings, paragraphs, lists, quotes, separators)

fixesApplied.description: Added missing `not-discourse` class to TSX wrapper
fixesApplied.resolution: Added `not-discourse` to useBlockProps className to match PHP template: `className: 'text-block py-10 sm:py-16 not-discourse'`

fixesApplied.description: Added shouldDisplay/hasContent conditional for ThemeHeading in TSX
fixesApplied.resolution: Imported shouldDisplay utility, computed hasContent boolean matching PHP logic (eyebrow || heading || description || buttons with url/postId), wrapped ThemeHeading in `{ (displayContent || hasContent) && ... }` conditional. This ensures the editor shows ThemeHeading when selected (for authoring) but hides it when deselected and empty (matching frontend PHP behavior).

Added comprehensive docblock at top of PHP file documenting all available variables with @var annotations: anchor, eyebrow, heading, description, buttons, children, block. Follows the same pattern as ContentWithMedia.php.

notes: The Text block is a straightforward two-column layout with ThemeHeading (sticky) on the left and InnerBlocks rich text on the right.
The PHP template already had the correct hasContent conditional logic - the TSX just needed to be updated to match it.
The shouldDisplay() utility ensures authoring UX is preserved: ThemeHeading is always visible when the block is selected (so authors can fill in content), but hidden when deselected and empty.
All 7 test page variations created successfully with DemoContainer wrappers for self-documentation.
Build, lint, and sync validation all pass with no errors related to the Text block.
